components: src-tauri/src/license.rs — License key validation and Ed25519 signature verification
src-tauri/src/ai_provider.rs — Claude API and Ollama HTTP dispatch
src-tauri/src/commands/ai.rs — Tauri IPC commands for AI analysis
src-tauri/src/commands/license.rs — Tauri IPC commands for license activation
backend/routes/auth.js — JWT registration and login endpoints
backend/server.js — Express server setup, CORS policy, JWT secret validation
in_scope.scope_restriction: Desktop application security: authentication, license enforcement, API key handling, input validation, SSRF in Ollama URL handling. Network attack surface is loopback-only (127.0.0.1:3001).

out_of_scope: UI/UX issues with no security impact
Rate limiting bypass via IPv6 vs IPv4 differences (informational only)
Missing HTTPS (loopback-only server, no transport encryption required)
Game capture logic (YOLO inference, killfeed OCR) — no security-sensitive data
Build toolchain or dependency vulnerabilities unrelated to the application logic
Physical access attacks (attacker has full local machine access)
conditional_scope: Tauri IPC privilege escalation — in scope if exploitable from the webview without user interaction
Ollama SSRF — in scope if the ollama_url allows reaching non-loopback internal services
JWT token storage in Tauri store — in scope if the store is readable by other processes

frontend_webview.rationale: The Tauri webview runs sandboxed React/TypeScript code. It can call Tauri IPC commands but cannot directly access Rust memory or the OS. Treat webview-sourced inputs as semi-trusted.

backend_local_api.rationale: The Express.js backend listens on 127.0.0.1:3001 only. Network requests come from the Tauri webview or the Rust layer. External network access is blocked by the loopback binding.

user_configuration.rationale: Values entered by the user (license keys, API keys, Ollama URL, recording output path) are untrusted and must be validated before use.

anthropic_api.rationale: Responses from the Anthropic Claude API are treated as trusted content for coaching, but the response structure must be validated before deserialization.

ollama_local.rationale: Ollama is a locally-running service. The URL is user-configurable, introducing SSRF risk if not validated.

severity_classification.CRITICAL: License bypass allowing unauthorized tier access in production builds; JWT secret exposure allowing token forgery; Claude API key exfiltration to external party
severity_classification.HIGH: SSRF via Ollama URL allowing internal network probing; SQL injection in auth routes; path traversal allowing write to system directories
severity_classification.MEDIUM: Rate limiting bypass; user enumeration via timing or differential responses; missing authentication on coaching endpoints
severity_classification.LOW: Debug-mode signature bypass (acceptable in debug builds); minor input validation gaps with no direct exploit path; missing error message sanitization
